<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell"><p class="eyebrow">Publications</p><h1>Research outputs</h1><p>Journal articles, working papers, a book chapter and data outputs. Newest first.</p></div></header>

</article></div></details><details class="publication-year-section" data-year-group="2025" open><summary><span>2025</span><small>5 articles</small></summary><div class="publication-year-records"><article class="publication-record" data-field="public-health" data-year="2025" data-type="journal" data-search="2025 are we ready for the next pandemic? public preferences and trade-offs between vaccine characteristics and societal restrictions across 21 countries marcello antonini; mesfin g. genie; katie attwell; arthur e. attema; jeremy k. ward; alessia melegaro; aleksandra torbica; brian kelly; chiara berardi; ana rita sequeira; n. mcgregor; a. kellner; shuli brammli-greenberg; madeleine hinwood; l. murauskiene; daiga behmane; zsolt j. balogh; terje p. hagen; francesco paolucci social science &amp; medicine 366, 117687 public health policy peer-reviewed journal articles published">
<h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2025.117687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Are We Ready for the Next Pandemic? Public Preferences and Trade-offs between Vaccine Characteristics and Societal Restrictions across 21 Countries</a></h3>
<div class="publication-authors">Marcello Antonini; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Katie Attwell; Arthur E. Attema; Jeremy K. Ward; Alessia Melegaro; Aleksandra Torbica; Brian Kelly; Chiara Berardi; Ana Rita Sequeira; N. McGregor; A. Kellner; Shuli Brammli-Greenberg; Madeleine Hinwood; L. Murauskiene; Daiga Behmane; Zsolt J. Balogh; Terje P. Hagen; Francesco Paolucci</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><span><em>Social Science &amp; Medicine</em>, 366, 117687</span><span class="status-tag">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2025.117687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">doi:10.1016/j.socscimed.2025.117687</a></div>
</article><article class="publication-record" data-field="public-health" data-year="2025" data-type="journal" data-search="2025 democracy, trust, and political orientation: disentangling mechanisms shaping individuals’ vaccine attitudes mesfin genie journal of health politics, policy and law  public health policy peer-reviewed journal articles published">
<h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1215/03616878-12166725" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Democracy, Trust, and Political Orientation: Disentangling Mechanisms Shaping Individuals’ Vaccine Attitudes</a></h3>
<div class="publication-authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong></div>

</article></div></details><details class="publication-year-section" data-year-group="2020"><summary><span>2020</span><small>3 articles</small></summary><div class="publication-year-records"><article class="publication-record" data-field="public-health" data-year="2020" data-type="journal" data-search="2020 the covid-19 pandemic in italy: policy and technology impact on health and non-health outcomes chiara berardi; marcello antonini; mesfin g. genie; g. cotugno; a. lanteri; adrian melia; francesco paolucci health policy and technology 9, 454-487 public health policy peer-reviewed journal articles published">
<h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2020.08.019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">The COVID-19 Pandemic in Italy: Policy and Technology Impact on Health and Non-health Outcomes</a></h3>
<div class="publication-authors">Chiara Berardi; Marcello Antonini; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; G. Cotugno; A. Lanteri; Adrian Melia; Francesco Paolucci</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><span><em>Health Policy and Technology</em>, 9, 454-487</span><span class="status-tag">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2020.08.019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">doi:10.1016/j.hlpt.2020.08.019</a> · <a href="https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.3680293" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Earlier version ↗</a></div>
</article><article class="publication-record" data-field="preference" data-year="2020" data-type="journal" data-search="2020 the role of heterogeneity of patients’ preferences in kidney transplantation mesfin g. genie; antonio nicolò; giacomo pasini journal of health economics 72, 102331 preferences and choice peer-reviewed journal articles published">
<h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">The Role of Heterogeneity of Patients’ Preferences in Kidney Transplantation</a></h3>
<div class="publication-authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Antonio Nicolò; Giacomo Pasini</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><span><em>Journal of Health Economics</em>, 72, 102331</span><span class="status-tag">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">doi:10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331</a> · <a href="https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.3428620" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Earlier version ↗</a></div>
</article><article class="publication-record" data-field="public-health" data-year="2020" data-type="journal" data-search="2020 understanding public preferences and trade-offs for government responses during a pandemic: a protocol for a discrete choice experiment in the uk mesfin g. genie; luis e. loría-rebolledo; shantini paranjothy; daniel powell; mandy ryan; ruben a. sakowsky; verity watson bmj open 10, e043477 public health policy peer-reviewed journal articles published">
<h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Understanding Public Preferences and Trade-offs for Government Responses during a Pandemic: A Protocol for a Discrete Choice Experiment in the UK</a></h3>
<div class="publication-authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Luis E. Loría-Rebolledo; Shantini Paranjothy; Daniel Powell; Mandy Ryan; Ruben A. Sakowsky; Verity Watson</div>
<div class="publication-meta"><span><em>BMJ Open</em>, 10, e043477</span><span class="status-tag">Published</span></div>
<div class="publication-links"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">doi:10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477</a></div>
</article></div></details></section><section class="publication-type-section" data-publication-section="chapter"><h2>Book chapter</h2><details class="publication-year-section" data-year-group="2025"><summary><span>2025</span><small>1 article</small></summary><div class="publication-year-records"><article class="publication-record" data-field="public-health" data-year="2025" data-type="chapter" data-search="2025 telehealth evolution and policy response in australia: insights from the covid-19 pandemic stefano bruzzo-gallardo; mesfin genie; r. gallagher; francesco paolucci information technologies in the healthcare industry 561-592 public health policy book chapter published">

</article></div></details></section><section class="publication-type-section" data-publication-section="working"><h2>Working papers</h2><details class="publication-year-section" data-year-group="2025"><summary><span>2025</span><small>1 article</small></summary><div class="publication-year-records"><article class="publication-record" data-field="public-health" data-year="2025" data-type="working" data-search="2025 vaccinating now or vaccinating later: separating pull-forward and net effects using a dynamic regression discontinuity design fabio i. martinenghi; mesfin genie; katie attwell; huong le; hannah c. moore; aregawi g. gebremariam; bette liu; francesco paolucci; christopher c. blyth arxiv  public health policy working papers working paper">
<h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.22714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Vaccinating Now or Vaccinating Later: Separating Pull-Forward and Net Effects Using a Dynamic Regression Discontinuity Design</a></h3>
<div class="publication-authors">Fabio I. Martinenghi; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Katie Attwell; Huong Le; Hannah C. Moore; Aregawi G. Gebremariam; Bette Liu; Francesco Paolucci; Christopher C. Blyth</div>
